extracted.brandBackground: Floor Coverings International operates a mobile retail floor covering business offering soft and hard flooring products directly to consumers.

leadership.name: Thomas W. Wood
leadership.background: Has served as President, CEO and Director of FCI since January 2005.
leadership.whyItMatters: Provides long-term executive leadership and stability to the franchise system.

leadership.name: Brian McDonough
leadership.background: Served as Treasurer, Secretary and Director since 2021; previously VP of Finance at FS Brands.
leadership.whyItMatters: Oversees financial controls and corporate governance across the brand portfolio.

leadership.name: Charles E. Chase
leadership.background: Director of FCI since August 2010 and President of FS Brands, Inc.
leadership.whyItMatters: Connects the franchise to parent company resources and strategic alignment.

leadership.name: Chad Schloerke
leadership.background: Joined FCI in August 2006 as a trainer and operations manager, currently COO.
leadership.whyItMatters: Directs franchisee support, training, and operational execution.

leadership.name: Benjamin Pace
leadership.background: Joined FCI in February 2020; previously Director of Accounting and Finance at Ballentine.
leadership.whyItMatters: Manages the financial strategy and accounting systems for the franchisor.

leadership.name: Albert Hermans
leadership.background: Joined FCI in June 2015 as Director of Emerging Operations, currently CDO.
leadership.whyItMatters: Leads franchise system expansion and recruitment efforts.

lineItems.notes: Travel, lodging, and meals for training.
Lease or purchase of branded vehicle.

hiddenCosts.description: Annual fee for proprietary software maintenance.

hiddenCosts.description: Annual cost to update flooring and window treatment samples.

operationalRisks.title: Out-of-State Dispute Resolution
operationalRisks.description: Disputes must be resolved by mediation or litigation only in Georgia.

operationalRisks.title: Mandatory Minimum Payments
operationalRisks.description: Franchisees must pay minimum royalty and advertising fees regardless of sales.

operationalRisks.title: Sales Performance Required
operationalRisks.description: Failure to maintain minimum sales levels can result in loss of territory or termination.

extracted.conceptRationale: The franchise operates as a mobile retail floor covering business providing home-based design consultations and installation services.
extracted.staffingRationale: The business is mobile-based and can be operated directly by the owner or with a small team of design associates and contracted installers.

riskReasons: 2 high-severity operational tripwires disclosed — see the tripwires section.
notes: Average rent not found; fixed costs may be understated.

underwriting.assessment: $250,000 liquid covers the $240,950 mid-point build-out without financing, though that leaves little working-capital cushion. At mid-cohort revenue, modeled cash flow after debt service is about $229,276/mo before payroll and maintenance — a thin margin of error.

insights.conceptRationale: The franchise operates as a mobile retail floor covering business providing home-based design consultations and installation services.

benchmark.label: Home & trade services (mobile / low-overhead)
benchmark.dominantRisk: Crew utilization, callback/warranty cost, and customer-acquisition cost. Low fixed overhead, but CAC and scheduling efficiency decide profitability.
benchmark.rampNote: 3–9 months to a steady job pipeline.
considerations: Budget materials/subcontractor COGS ~25–40% and field labor ~20–35%.
CAC (lead cost per booked job) is the dominant hidden line — ask Item 20 operators what they actually spend per job.
Confirm territory size, lead-generation expectations, and any required call-center/marketing fees.

crossCheck.message: No operating-EBITDA margin is disclosed in Item 19. The 10–25% band below is an industry estimate for this concept — validate it against real franchisee P&Ls (Item 20) before relying on any margin.

insights.staffingNote: Flagged as an lightly staffed model from the FDD, so labor is adjusted down to 10–21% — the 20–35% category band assumes a staffed venue. Confirm actual staffing with Item 20 franchisees.

buildup.label: − Occupancy / rent (not disclosed)
buildup.note: Item 7 disclosed no rent figure — benchmark occupancy applied so the line above isn't overstated

buildup.label: − Labor
buildup.note: lightly staffed · ≈ 7.7–16.2 FTE at ~$20/hr fully loaded

assumptions.detail: Classified as “Home & trade services (mobile / low-overhead)” from the FDD (Item 1 / brand description).
Read as lightly staffed from the FDD, which lowers the labor band applied below.
Item 7 disclosed no rent — occupancy estimated at the 2–6% category band.
Estimated at the 25–40% category band — COGS is never disclosed in an FDD.
Estimated at the 10–21% lightly staffed band — labor is never disclosed in an FDD.
Utilities, insurance, and R&M estimated at 6–10% (category catch-all).
Our calculation — the disclosed top line minus the benchmarked costs above. Not a figure stated in the FDD.

insights.consultCtaPitch: These are category benchmarks applied to the disclosed top line — your unit's real costs will differ, and that difference is the conversation. Want them pressure-tested against actual franchisee P&Ls for your territory?
insights.asOf: 2026 — general industry benchmarks, refine against real franchisee P&Ls
insights.disclaimer: Industry benchmark ranges for your own budgeting and Item 20 questions — NOT a projection of this franchise's results, and not investment advice.

financialCondition.headline: The franchisor's statements don't show the distress signals we screen for — positive net worth and a clean audit. Still worth a quick confirmation against current figures.
body: Why it matters to you: field support, training, technology, and marketing come out of the franchisor's budget. If outside funding slows, those are the first things to get cut. FS Brands, Inc. guarantees the franchisor's performance, which partly backstops this.

Verify before you commit: ask for the most recent interim (unaudited) financials and the current-year funding plan; ask whether the parent will sign a guarantee of performance; and cross-check Item 20 — unit closures, transfers, and terminations are the operational tell that corroborates financial stress.
aggravators: elevated unit closures (25 in the last year, about 8% of the system)
mitigants: the auditor issued a clean (unmodified) opinion and did not raise going-concern doubt
the parent guarantees the franchisor's performance
financialCondition.evidenceNote: Derived from the Item 21 / Exhibit F audited financials. Figures are extracted; all ratios and grading are computed. Confirm against the source statements and a CPA before relying on them.
